title: lattice-health
description: The verifier for a lattice's STRUCTURAL health — the grid as one artifact, not any single cell's content. Grades whether the lattice-architect's decomposition is honest, partial-order-legal, and free of un-propagated staleness, and whether the trajectory rule was respected (no grid-filling). Gate dimensions are mechanical (computable from lattice.json by lattice.py check); review dimensions need a calibrated critic that is not the architect that drew the grid. This rubric cell must itself be `validated` before any lattice binds to it (the verifier-maturity precondition).
maturity_precondition: This rubric cell must be validated (deterministic across repeated runs on a fixed lattice.json; calibrated against the few-shot exemplar grids) before it gates any lattice.

calibration.note: Few-shot scored lattices spanning the scale — an honest depth-first slice, an over-green grid-fill, a partial-order inversion, an un-propagated staleness — with reference scores. An uncalibrated judge drifts generous toward a full-looking grid.

pristine_reference.note: Maturity claims are scored against the ledger (a maturity is real only if a signal transition records it), not against the architect's stub. The ledger is read-only to the architect — a pristine reference it cannot reach.

dimensions.check: Every cell at `validated` or above has a corresponding critic signal recorded in the ledger; no cell is seeded above the maturity its signals earn. A fresh slice is `absent`/`defined`.
dimensions.fail_meaning: A green grid the factory has not earned — the canonical grid-filling reward-hack at the lattice level.

dimensions.check: Every `depends_on` edge respects the layer partial order (ontology+spec -> rubric/policy/capability -> methodology/protocol -> ledger -> pattern); no cycle. Computed by lattice.py.
dimensions.fail_meaning: An upside-down edge (a rubric depended on by its own spec) scores vibes and lattice.py cannot rank against it.

dimensions.check: Every cell's `validated_against` upstream hashes still match the current upstream content hashes; no dependent that should be `stale` is silently still `validated`.
dimensions.fail_meaning: A cell trusted against an upstream that has since changed — a silent false-validated, the staleness cascade never run.

dimensions.check: No cell ID encodes maturity/state; every ID is {layer}.{scope}.{slug}. A cell was never renamed on a transition.
dimensions.fail_meaning: Identity-encodes-state is a drift generator and breaks validated_against hashes.

dimensions.check: Does the layer×scope decomposition cover the domain's real modalities, or are whole layers (rubric, policy, protocol) silently missing — a working demo with no verifiers?
dimensions.fail_meaning: A grid missing modalities is the hacker pathology: depth without the layers that make it trustworthy.

dimensions.check: Is the grid a depth-first vertical slice toward `validated`, or breadth-first at `defined` (everything specified, nothing real)? Was the modality axis rescanned at each widening?
dimensions.fail_meaning: Breadth-first at `defined` is the enterprise-architecture pathology — a fully-drawn grid with no signal anywhere.

dimensions.check: Is each cell placed at the RIGHT scope — the smallest grain that yields decisive signal — rather than inflated to system/fleet to look ambitious?
dimensions.fail_meaning: A mis-scoped cell probes at the wrong grain: too coarse thrashes, too fine never generalizes.

scoring.gates: ALL gate dimensions must pass; any gate failure returns the lattice to `regenerating` regardless of review score — a structurally broken grid does not score on taste.
scoring.review: Weighted review score must clear each dimension threshold AND an aggregate of 80/100.
scoring.signal: On pass, the critic (not the lattice-architect) writes signals/{ontology-cell}/{ts}--lattice-health.json. The architect cannot write this path (gate-signal).
